MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-13-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. SIMON MAZZOLLA of the Mazzolla Law Office, Tel No. (650) 403-4802 appearing for Plaintiff Graham Schneider;
Atty. LENORE C. KELLY of Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz, Edelman & Dicker, LLP, Tel No. (415) 433-0990 appearing for defendants Ann Sacks Tile & Stone, Inc., and Jeff Fountain.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ANN SACKS TILE & STONE, INC. AND JEFF FOUNTAIN'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is granted with 10 days leave to amend. The Complaint fails to state a cause of action for fraud (see ruling on the Demurrer), and no other allegations in the Complaint are sufficient to support a claim for punitive damages. DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-13-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. SIMON MAZZOLLA of the Mazzolla Law Office, Tel No. (650) 403-4802 appearing for Plaintiff Graham Schneider;
Atty. LENORE C. KELLY of Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz, Edelman & Dicker, LLP, Tel No. (415) 433-0990 appearing for defendants Ann Sacks Tile & Stone, Inc., and Jeff Fountain.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ANN SACKS TILE & STONE, INC. AND JEFF FOUNTAIN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part. As to the fraud cause of action, the Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to permit Plaintiff the opportunity to meet the heightened pleading requirements for fraud causes of action. (See Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 49 Cal.4th 631, 645 [In asserting a fraud claim against a corporate employer, a plaintiff must "allege the names of the persons who made the allegedly fraudulent representations, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written."].) As to the cause of action for unfair competition, the Demurrer is overruled, as Plaintiff sufficiently alleges a claim under the unfairness prong, which is all that is required. (See In re Toyota Motor Corp. Unintended Acceleration Marketing (C.D. Cal. 2010) 754 F.Supp.2d 1145, 1175 [a plaintiff may pursue a UCL claim under any or all three theories].) Finally, as to the breach of written contract claim, the Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend for Plaintiff to adequately allege the terms of the contract or the legal intendment and effect of the contract. (See Construction Protective Services, Inc. v. TIG Specialty Ins. Co. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 189, 198-199.) DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)
